note: AG-PROMPT-392 canonical extension-description alignment. Executes the AG-391-deferred (DEFER_DESCRIPTION_ALIGNMENT) work while PRESERVING version 1.0.1 -- no 1.0.1 package has been uploaded to the Chrome Web Store, so the corrected-description package may retain 1.0.1. One approved canonical extension description was established (exactly 129 ASCII chars, within the 132-char browser-manifest limit): 'Warns before sharing sensitive text or files with supported AI chat sites. Runs locally in your browser. No account or telemetry.' It was applied to four canonical metadata sources -- public/manifest.chrome.json (.description), public/manifest.firefox.json (.description), public/_locales/en/messages.json (extensionDescription.message), and package.json (.description; the description-only edit does not touch package-lock.json, which has no description key). The legacy public/manifest.json (v0.2.0, wildcard hosts) was left unchanged and proven dead/excluded (never built or packaged). A dev-only automated consistency test (scripts/test-extension-description-consistency.ts, 27 checks) guards against future drift and is intentionally NOT promoted to this production release. No icon/permission/host/CSP/runtime/detection/policy/UI change; approved icon bytes unchanged and re-verified byte-exact at source, Chrome build, Firefox build, and final ZIP (icon16 e562af70..., icon32 02cf5879..., icon48 6fad62a2..., icon128 5a3f08a1...). Chrome ZIP compared file-by-file against the superseded AG-391 ZIP (8cb7de15...): 11 files/14 entries both, none added/removed, EXACTLY TWO files differ -- manifest.json (only .description; version still 1.0.1) and _locales/en/messages.json (only extensionDescription.message); content.js, background.js, popup.html, warning-modal.css, warning-modal.html, and all four icons are byte-identical. The release clone was renormalized to canonical LF-committed bytes (working-tree-only core.autocrlf=false/core.eol=lf, files re-materialized from their own LF blobs -- no git history/blob change) so the release-side deterministic build/package reproduced the dev staging ZIP byte-for-byte. Dev gates: tsc clean, description-consistency 27/27, icon-integrity 66/66, CORE 56/56, verify:manifest 28/28, taxonomy 0 FAIL/2 pre-existing WARN, Chrome build (artifacts + 20/20 consumer-copy guard), Firefox build 25/25. Release gates: release-verify.ts 10/10. Chrome MV3 invariants reconfirmed: permissions=[storage] only, exactly the 6 AI host patterns, no wildcard/<all_urls>, connect-src none, no remote code. No Chrome Web Store upload; no GitHub Release; no git tag; no store-listing or dashboard-field change (listing description NOT auto-changed). Original repositories untouched. Package status READY_FOR_OPERATOR_SMOKE pending manual operator verification.
